options.text: Overtake only when it's clear of oncoming traffic

options.label: B
options.text: Overtake on the right if safe

options.label: C
options.text: Ensure you signal before turning left too

question: You want to overtake a vehicle in a tunnel. What must you do?

options.label: B
options.text: Evaluate if the tunnel is well-lit

options.label: C
options.text: Proceed only if traffic is light

question: You want to change lanes near an exit. What must you do?

options.label: B
options.text: Rely on the cooperation of other drivers

question: You want to change lanes in fog. What must you do?

options.label: A
options.text: Proceed as normal; fog doesn't affect lane changing

question: You are driving at 50 km/h. Approximately how long is your reaction distance (assuming 1 second reaction time)?

question: The general speed limit inside German towns is 50 km/h. When may you drive faster?

options.label: A
options.text: Only when a higher limit is explicitly signed

options.label: B
options.text: On wide roads with no pedestrians

question: On a rural road outside a town, what is the standard speed limit for cars in Germany?

options.label: A

options.label: B

options.label: C

question: You are driving at 50 km/h and need to brake suddenly. Approximately how long is the total stopping distance on a dry road?

question: You want to drive 20 km/h through a residential play street (Spielstraße). What is the actual speed limit there?
On a wet road, how does braking distance change compared to dry conditions?

options.label: A

question: A child runs onto the road 20 m ahead while you are driving at 50 km/h. Can you stop in time?

options.label: A
options.text: Yes, total stopping distance at 50 km/h is about 25 m on dry road

options.label: B
options.text: No, you need at least 40 m to stop at that speed

options.label: C
options.text: Yes, modern ABS halves the stopping distance to 10 m

question: You are driving at 130 km/h on the Autobahn and need to stop in an emergency. Approximately how long is your stopping distance?
In a 30 km/h zone, a pedestrian steps out 15 m ahead. Can you stop in time on a dry road?

options.label: A
options.text: No, you need at least 30 m at that speed

options.label: B
options.text: Yes, stopping distance at 30 km/h is approximately 13 m

options.label: C
options.text: Only if you have ABS fitted

question: What speed limit applies to cars towing a trailer over 750 kg on German motorways?

options.label: B

options.label: C

question: You are approaching a hazard at 100 km/h. A driver ahead brakes suddenly. Why is a large following distance critical?

options.label: A
options.text: Because reaction time alone uses about 28 m before braking even starts

options.label: B
options.text: Because it is required only when driving above the advisory speed

options.label: C
options.text: Because ABS does not work at speeds above 80 km/h
